title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT)
subtitle: Focus on psychological flexibility: where avoidance and fusion narrow values-based action.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: Identify the experiential avoidance pattern: What is the client avoiding? What cognitive fusion is maintaining this? What values are being neglected?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Adlerian Therapy
subtitle: Focus on lifestyle, belonging, and discouragement shaping goals of behavior.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: What is the client's lifestyle pattern? How do early recollections, birth order, and social interest shape their current difficulties?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
subtitle: Focus on thoughts, emotions, behaviors, and core beliefs driving the cycle.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: Identify the core belief → intermediate belief → automatic thought chain. What cognitive distortions maintain the problem? What behavioral patterns reinforce it?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR)
subtitle: Focus on unprocessed memories, present triggers, and negative cognitions.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: What is the target memory? What negative cognition is associated? What is the desired positive cognition? Rate SUD and VOC.

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Internal Family Systems (IFS)
subtitle: Focus on parts roles, burdens, and building Self-leadership.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: Which parts are active? Identify protectors (managers/firefighters) and exiles. What is the Self's relationship to these parts?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Motivational Interviewing (MI)
subtitle: Focus on ambivalence, change talk, readiness, and confidence.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: Where is the client on the stages of change? What sustain talk vs. change talk do you hear? What discrepancy exists between values and behavior?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Narrative Therapy
subtitle: Focus on dominant stories, language, and preferred identity.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: What is the dominant problem-saturated story? What unique outcomes or exceptions exist? What alternative story is emerging?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: Solution-Focused Brief Therapy (SFBT)
subtitle: Focus on preferred future, exceptions, and strengths.

fields.name: Client + Context
fields.prompt: Age, pronouns, presenting issue, referral source, relevant background (cultural, developmental, relational).

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, functional impact. What brought the client in now?

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis (What is maintaining the problem?)
fields.prompt: What does the client's preferred future look like? What exceptions to the problem already exist? On a scale of 1–10, where are they now?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes, patterns, or behaviors will treatment target? Be concrete and measurable.

fields.name: Protective Factors + Strengths
fields.prompt: Existing resources: social support, coping skills, motivation, cultural strengths, past successes.

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Practice
fields.prompt: Specific tasks the client will practice between sessions. Include frequency and tracking method.

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know treatment is working? Include both subjective markers and objective measures (e.g., PHQ-9 scores).

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, contraindications, barriers to treatment, cultural considerations, ethical issues.

title: DBT Case Conceptualization Worksheet
subtitle: Dialectical Behavior Therapy — One-page formulation for supervision and case conferences

fields.name: Client & Context
fields.prompt: Name/ID, date, session number, and relevant contextual information.

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, and functional impact.

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis
fields.prompt: How does this modality explain the presenting problem? What maintains it?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes or patterns need to shift for improvement?

fields.name: Protective Factors & Strengths
fields.prompt: What resources, supports, and resilience factors does the client bring?

fields.name: Intervention Plan
fields.prompt: Which specific techniques will you use and in what sequence?

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Tasks
fields.prompt: What practice or reflection will the client do between sessions?

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know the intervention is working? What will change?

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, cultural factors, potential barriers to treatment.

title: Psychodynamic Case Conceptualization Worksheet
subtitle: Psychodynamic Therapy — One-page formulation for supervision and case conferences

fields.name: Client & Context
fields.prompt: Name/ID, date, session number, and relevant contextual information.

fields.name: Presenting Concerns
fields.prompt: Primary symptoms, duration, severity, and functional impact.

fields.name: Lens Hypothesis
fields.prompt: How does this modality explain the presenting problem? What maintains it?

fields.name: Change Targets
fields.prompt: What specific processes or patterns need to shift for improvement?

fields.name: Protective Factors & Strengths
fields.prompt: What resources, supports, and resilience factors does the client bring?

fields.name: Intervention Plan
fields.prompt: Which specific techniques will you use and in what sequence?

fields.name: Homework / Between-Session Tasks
fields.prompt: What practice or reflection will the client do between sessions?

fields.name: Progress Indicators
fields.prompt: How will you know the intervention is working? What will change?

fields.name: Risks / Considerations
fields.prompt: Safety concerns, cultural factors, potential barriers to treatment.

fields.name: Warning Signs
fields.prompt: Thoughts, images, moods, situations, or behaviors that indicate a crisis may be developing.

fields.name: Internal Coping Strategies
fields.prompt: Things I can do on my own to take my mind off problems without contacting another person (e.g., relaxation, exercise, hobbies).

fields.name: People & Social Settings That Provide Distraction
fields.prompt: People I can contact or places I can go to distract myself (name, phone number).

fields.name: People I Can Ask for Help
fields.prompt: Family members or friends I can reach out to when in crisis (name, phone number).

fields.name: Professional Supports
fields.prompt: Clinician name and phone, local urgent care, crisis team contact information.

fields.name: Emergency Services
fields.prompt: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, 911, local emergency room address and phone, Crisis Text Line (text HOME to 741741).

fields.name: Means Safety Plan
fields.prompt: Steps to reduce access to lethal means. Who will secure firearms, medications, or other means? Where will they be stored?

fields.name: Reasons for Living
fields.prompt: The most important things to me that are worth living for (people, pets, goals, beliefs, responsibilities).

fields.name: My Environment Safe — Next 24 Hours
fields.prompt: Specific plan for the next 24 hours: where I'll be, who I'll be with, what I'll do, when I'll check in.
